skill.name: optimizing-performance
skill.description: Analyzes and optimizes application performance across frontend, backend, and database layers. Use when diagnosing slowness, improving load times, optimizing queries, reducing bundle size, or when asked about performance issues.
skill.summary: Guides measured performance optimization across frontend, backend, and database layers.
skill.author: CloudAI-X

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic imports, shell-like backticks, a hardcoded URL, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are documentation examples in SKILL.md, including Markdown fences, JavaScript template literals, SQL examples, and a placeholder Lighthouse URL. No evidence found of prompt injection, hidden execution, credential access, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown and Template Literal False Positives
low_findings.description: The shell backtick detections are Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals inside SKILL.md examples. The skill file does not define executable scripts or a command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visibly fenced documentation blocks or string interpolation examples. No executable wrapper, installation hook, or automation file is present.

low_findings.title: Instructional Profiling Commands
low_findings.description: The Node.js, Python, and Lighthouse commands are examples for users measuring their own applications. They are not invoked automatically by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear under a Profiling Commands heading in a Markdown document. The file contains guidance only, so execution requires an explicit user action.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Network URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is https://example.com in a Lighthouse command example. It is a placeholder target, not an exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL uses example.com in an instructional benchmark command. No secret values, file contents, or environment variables are sent to it.

low_findings.title: Unconfirmed Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert at the skill description and reconnaissance alerts on SQL SELECT examples have no supporting malicious context. The SQL appears in database optimization guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic API or host reconnaissance command is present at the cited locations. The SQL lines are generic query optimization examples.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Used for Code Splitting
low_findings.description: The dynamic import expression is shown as a React lazy-loading pattern. It is a frontend optimization example and does not load remote or user-controlled code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import target is a local component path inside a code sample. There is no evidence of dynamic remote module loading or unsanitized input.

content.value_statement: Slow applications lose users and make production issues harder to diagnose. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a measured workflow for profiling, improving, and validating performance.
actual_capabilities: Provides a baseline, bottleneck, optimization, and remeasurement workflow.
Lists profiling commands for Node.js, Python, and web performance checks.
Explains frontend improvements for bundle size, rendering, and images.
Shows backend patterns for query optimization, caching, and async processing.
Defines target metrics for Web Vitals and API latency.
Includes validation checks for comparing results after changes.
limitations: Does not run profilers or collect metrics automatically.
Requires users to adapt examples to their application stack.
Does not provide tool-specific setup for every framework or database.
Does not replace load testing or production observability.

use_cases.title: Reduce Page Load Time
use_cases.description: Audit bundle size, image loading, rendering cost, and Web Vitals before choosing optimizations.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Slow APIs
use_cases.description: Profile backend latency, identify blocking work, and compare P50, P95, and P99 response times.

use_cases.title: Improve Database Access
use_cases.description: Find N+1 queries, review joins and pagination, and decide where caching can reduce repeated reads.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a performance review checklist for my application. Focus on baseline metrics, likely bottlenecks, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Frontend Slowness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my frontend performance issues. Prioritize bundle size, rendering behavior, image loading, and Web Vitals targets.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Backend Latency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze backend latency. Help me inspect database queries, caching options, blocking work, and API latency metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Measurement Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build a before-and-after measurement plan for a performance project. Include metrics, profiling tools, expected changes, and regression checks.

output_examples.input: My React dashboard is slow on first load.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering bundle size, route-level loading, heavy components, image strategy, and Web Vitals targets.

output_examples.input: Our user list endpoint slows down as data grows.
output_examples.output: A backend checklist that checks query shape, pagination, indexes, caching layers, and latency percentiles.

output_examples.input: We optimized a job processor but need proof it helped.
output_examples.output: A validation plan comparing baseline and post-change metrics, checking regressions, and documenting improvement percentages.

best_practices: Measure baseline performance before changing code.
Optimize the largest proven bottleneck first.
Repeat the same measurements after each change.
anti_patterns: Chasing average latency while ignoring high percentiles.
Adding cache layers without invalidation and memory limits.

faq.question: Does this skill run performance tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and example commands that users must adapt and run themselves.

faq.question: Can it help with frontend and backend issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers frontend rendering, bundles, images, backend latency, database queries, caching, and async work.

faq.question: Which metrics does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes Web Vitals, API latency percentiles, error rate, before-and-after comparisons, and regression checks.

faq.question: Is it useful before production incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps teams prepare measurable targets and repeatable checks before performance issues become urgent.

faq.question: Does it replace observability platforms?
faq.answer: No. It complements observability by helping interpret metrics and choose targeted code or architecture changes.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow starts with baseline measurement and explains common bottleneck categories before suggesting optimizations.
